# Tasks: #208 — Build review-pr high-order play with diff-scoped quality standards, severity taxonomy, and reviewer selection
<!-- Generated by start-feature-planning | Plan sub-agent output -->

**Intent:** Implement the spec via granular, dependency-ordered tasks that respect the IDD compile pipeline and avoid chicken-and-egg problems with `/create-play`.

**Constraints:**
- Build order must ensure `quality-check-scoped` exists before `/create-play --bake review-pr` runs (create-play Step 3 gate).
- `ship` is re-baked AFTER `review-pr` is compiled (ship's sub-play chain references review-pr).
- `/sync-claude` runs once at the end, not per task.
- `create-pr` is not modified.

**Failure Conditions:**
- A task runs out of order and a downstream `/create-play` invocation fails its existence gates.
- `ship` is re-baked before `review-pr` exists.
- `idsd.md` updated before all artifacts land (stale claim of completeness).

## Dependency Graph

```
T1 (taxonomy)
  └─► T2 (config)
        └─► T3 (quality-check-scoped skill)
              └─► T4 (review-pr intent.yaml)
                    └─► T5 (/create-play --bake review-pr)
                          └─► T6 (ship intent.yaml delta)
                                └─► T7 (/create-play --rebake ship)
                                      └─► T8 (idsd.md updates)
                                            └─► T9 (/sync-claude)
                                                  └─► T10 (end-to-end smoke test)
```

## Task Breakdown

### T1: Author PR severity taxonomy
- **Files:** `core/components/memory/standards/pr-severity-taxonomy.md` (create)
- **Details:** Table-form standard. Header explains mechanical-only rule: every row must have `| Standard ID | Severity (P1-P4) | Match Rule | Evidence Required |`. Populate from existing KB domain files: walk `core/components/memory/knowledge/quality/{code,security,architecture,testing,documentation,operations,frontend,backend,data,performance,tech-debt}/*.md`, extract all `XXX-NN` IDs, assign severities by category:
  - **P1 (blocker):** SEC-* auth/secret checks, ARCH-* API contract breaks, SEC-* injection/OWASP, DATA-* migration-safety
  - **P2 (major):** CODE-* lint errors, TEST-* missing unit test for new code, BE-* error-handling violations, PERF-* load-critical regressions
  - **P3 (minor):** DOC-* missing docstrings, CODE-* complexity borderline, FE-* a11y
  - **P4 (nit):** DEBT-* TODOs, style-only CODE-*
  Each row's Match Rule is a grep/path pattern. Add explicit note: "No LLM judgment — if no rule matches, no finding".
- **Depends on:** none
- **Expected Outcome:** Deterministic mapping from existing standard IDs to P1..P4 buckets.
- **Verification:** V1 — grep shows every row begins with real standard ID; spot-check 10 rules for mechanical form.

### T2: Extend core/config.yaml
- **Files:** `core/config.yaml` (modify)
- **Details:** Add after `stm:` block:
  ```yaml
  # Standards resolution (first play-specific section; pattern for future plays)
  standards:
    pr-severity-taxonomy: ./core/components/memory/standards/pr-severity-taxonomy.md

  review-pr:
    bypass: false
    confidence_threshold: 0.5
    reviewer_count: 3
    standards_order: [kb, ltm, stm]
    fallback_reviewers: []
    activity_window_days: 90
  ```
- **Depends on:** T1
- **Expected Outcome:** Config readable by pre-flight, taxonomy resolvable by path.
- **Verification:** V3 — all 6 keys present with declared defaults.

### T3: Create `quality-check-scoped` skill
- **Files:**
  - `core/components/skills/quality-check-scoped/SKILL.md` (create via `/skill-creator`)
  - `core/components/skills/quality-check-scoped/reference/input-contract.md` (create)
  - `core/components/skills/quality-check-scoped/reference/finding-schema.md` (create)
- **Details:** Invoke `/skill-creator` with purpose: "Diff-bounded single-pass quality evaluation for PR review. Reads KB standards from `standards_order` paths resolved from config, filters to domains touched by `changed_paths`, evaluates each relevant standard-ID check against the diff, classifies every finding via `pr-severity-taxonomy.md`, emits `findings.yaml`."
  - **Input contract:** `diff_path`, `changed_paths[]`, `standards_set{kb,ltm,stm paths}`, `severity_taxonomy_path`, `intent_summary`, `output_path`.
  - **Output schema per finding:** `{standard_id, severity, file, line, evidence, taxonomy_rule_id}`. Findings without `standard_id` rejected with skill-level hard error (F3 enforcement).
  - **Execution:** single pass. Reuses KB files but not quality-check's 11-subagent execution model.
  - **Explicit constraint:** "NEVER reads files outside `changed_paths`." Document as "Diff scope invariant."
- **Depends on:** T1, T2
- **Expected Outcome:** Skill exists with valid SKILL.md contract; create-play Step 3 skill-existence gate passes for review-pr.
- **Verification:** V4 — skill contract schema complete and enforces F3.

### T4: Author review-pr intent.yaml
- **Files:** `core/components/plays/review-pr/reference/intent.yaml` (create)
- **Details:** Invoke `intent-crafter` via `/create-play` (New mode; stop after Step 2 Intent gate). Content seed:
  - **intent:** "Review a pull request against diff-scoped quality standards resolved from config, classify findings deterministically via the PR severity taxonomy, gate on a confidence score, select the top N reviewers from recent committers on changed paths, and post structured routing to the PR."
  - **constraints:**
    - C1 Diff-scoped evaluation only; never full-repo.
    - C2 Every finding cites a standard ID from the KB.
    - C3 Severity classification is deterministic per `pr-severity-taxonomy.md`.
    - C4 `standards_order` and all thresholds come from `core/config.yaml`.
    - C5 ≤5 domain agents.
    - C6 Reviewer selection excludes the PR author and uses `activity_window_days` from config.
    - C7 Falls through to `fallback_reviewers` when fewer than `reviewer_count` candidates.
    - C8 PR comment uses a structured template; existing prior-run comment is updated, not stacked.
    - C9 `confidence = 0.25*scan_coverage + 0.35*citation_rate + 0.20*intent_alignment + 0.20*precedent_match`.
    - C10 `create-pr` is never invoked or modified from this play.
  - **failure_conditions:**
    - F1 Finding emitted without a standard ID.
    - F2 Full-repo quality-check executed.
    - F3 Reviewer selection returns <`reviewer_count` and no fallback configured.
    - F4 PR author included in selected reviewers.
    - F5 P1 finding present but PR not set to `request-changes`.
    - F6 Confidence < threshold but no reviewers requested.
    - F7 Severity classification non-deterministic across identical runs.
    - F8 `pr-severity-taxonomy.md` or config keys missing at pre-flight.
    - F9 Play exceeded L2 domain-agent budget.
  - **scenarios:**
    - S1 Reviewer: opens the PR and sees a structured comment with P1..P3 sections and confidence score.
    - S2 Author: can trace every finding to a cited standard ID and evidence line.
    - S3 Ship pipeline: P1 or low-confidence halts ship before merge.
    - S4 Determinism: two runs produce identical buckets.
- **Depends on:** T3
- **Expected Outcome:** intent.yaml schema-valid; user Tether on intent gate.
- **Verification:** schema check via create-play Step 2 gate.

### T5: Compile review-pr play
- **Files:**
  - `core/components/plays/review-pr/SKILL.md` (create via `/create-play --bake review-pr`)
  - STM artifacts under `{stm_base}/{issue}/evidence/create-play/review-pr/`
- **Details:** Run `/create-play --bake review-pr`. Skill Inventory Step 3 sees `quality-check-scoped` and passes. Agent Audit Step 4 covers `repo-orchestrator`, `tech-designer`, `quality-auditor` — all pre-existing. Workflow structure = **A** (checkpoint-capable, multi-agent, confidence-gated). Pre-flight picks up C4 (config resolution) and C1 (diff scope). evals-creator generates SE-1..SE-n and SCE-1..SCE-4 covering F1..F9 and S1..S4. Do NOT hand-edit compiled SKILL.md.
- **Depends on:** T4
- **Expected Outcome:** Compiled `review-pr/SKILL.md` with valid intent_hash, ≤5 domain agents, 6 workflow steps matching design.
- **Verification:** V5 — `/create-play --review review-pr` returns 11/11 PASS; V6 — domain agent count ≤5.

### T6: Update ship intent.yaml for bypass-conditional gate
- **Files:** `core/components/plays/ship/reference/intent.yaml` (modify)
- **Details:**
  - Replace "No human approvals at any stage — all decisions auto-proceed regardless of confidence level." with: *"Auto-proceeds end-to-end unless the `review-pr` quality gate blocks. When `review-pr.bypass` is false in `core/config.yaml`, `review-pr` runs between `create-pr` and `merge-pr`; a P1 finding or sub-threshold confidence halts the pipeline before merge. Set `bypass: true` to preserve the unconditional auto-proceed behavior."*
  - Soften C2: `All chained operations execute without human approval gates, EXCEPT the review-pr quality gate when review-pr.bypass is false.`
  - Add **C7**: `When review-pr.bypass is false, review-pr must run after create-pr and before merge-pr, and its block/escalate verdict must halt the ship pipeline.`
  - Add **F7**: `Ship completed a merge while review-pr.bypass was false and review-pr returned a P1 finding or sub-threshold confidence.`
  - Add **S3**: Code Reviewer persona — `Given the PR immediately before merge, can see the review-pr gate outcome (findings + confidence + reviewers) as a structured comment.`
- **Depends on:** T5
- **Expected Outcome:** Ship intent reflects new bypass-conditional gate.
- **Verification:** intent.yaml schema check; diff inspection.

### T7: Re-bake ship
- **Files:** `core/components/plays/ship/SKILL.md` (regenerate via `/create-play --rebake ship`)
- **Details:** `/create-play --rebake ship`. Compiled output should:
  - Add conditional Step 2.5 "Quality Gate" to sub-play chain table, gated on `bypass`.
  - Add pre-flight row "Resolve `review-pr.bypass` from `core/config.yaml`".
  - Add new evals for C7/F7/S3.
  - Update status-file `tasks` object to include conditional `review-pr: {status, bypass}` entry.
  - New intent_hash.
- **Depends on:** T6
- **Expected Outcome:** Fresh `ship/SKILL.md` that halts correctly when bypass=false and P1/low-confidence.
- **Verification:** V7 — `/create-play --review ship` returns 11/11 PASS with fresh intent_hash.

### T8: Update idsd.md
- **Files:** `.claude/specs/idsd/idsd.md` (modify)
- **Details:**
  - P13 row: `| P13 | review-pr | ✅ COMPLETE | L2, diff-scoped quality review with deterministic severity taxonomy, confidence gate, and git-history reviewer selection. Replaces validator-agent dependency with quality-auditor + taxonomy. |`
  - Remove validator-agent line from Dependency Blockers.
  - Add new subsection under Play Specs — Code-2-Test for `review-pr` describing the 6 workflow steps.
  - Under `ship` row, append: `Updated #208 (2026-04): adds conditional review-pr quality gate between create-pr and merge-pr when review-pr.bypass=false.`
  - Component Inventory: bump plays count; add `review-pr` and `quality-check-scoped` to deployed lists.
- **Depends on:** T7
- **Expected Outcome:** IDSD reflects P13 complete and ship's new conditional behavior.
- **Verification:** V14 — grep confirms P13 COMPLETE and validator-blocker line gone.

### T9: Sync to Claude
- **Files:** `.claude/skills/**`, `.claude/agents/**` (regenerated by /sync-claude)
- **Details:** Run `/sync-claude` once. Confirm `review-pr`, `quality-check-scoped`, updated `ship`, and (unchanged) `create-pr` all sync without errors.
- **Depends on:** T8
- **Expected Outcome:** Deployed inventory contains new play + skill; ship is re-baked version.
- **Verification:** V15 — sync output shows no errors and new components listed.

### T10: End-to-end smoke test
- **Files:** none (operational run on disposable test branch)
- **Details:** Create disposable test branch with three seeded variants:
  - (a) clean diff (doc typo fix) — expect auto-proceed through ship
  - (b) seeded P1 (new endpoint without auth middleware matching SEC-* taxonomy row) — expect ship halt with review-pr request-changes + reviewers
  - (c) seeded low-confidence (change with zero standard-ID citations possible, e.g., config-only tweak) — expect ship halt with reviewers requested even without P1
  Then re-run case (b) to verify determinism (identical findings).
- **Depends on:** T9
- **Expected Outcome:** All three variants behave per spec; determinism re-run identical.
- **Verification:** V2, V9, V10, V11, V12.
